steps.title: Identify the two possessives
steps.content: The sentence compares sample 15 and sample 18, so samples must be plural possessive. It also refers to the primary descriptors belonging to the odors, so odors must be plural possessive too.

steps.title: Reject incomplete forms
steps.content: Choice A makes sample singular. Choice B makes samples possessive but incorrectly treats odor as singular possessive. Choice C leaves samples nonpossessive, so it does not show that the odors belong to the samples.

steps.title: Use plural possessive endings
steps.content: Both plural nouns end in s, so each takes an apostrophe after the s: samples' odors' primary descriptors. The answer is D.
